question: Why is using semantic elements like <main>, <article>, and <nav> generally better than using only <div> tags?
options: Semantic tags always load faster in every browser
Semantic tags improve meaning, accessibility, and maintainability
Semantic tags automatically apply Bootstrap styles
Semantic tags remove the need for CSS classes

question: Which statement about the alt attribute on is most accurate?
options: It is only useful for search engines
It should repeat the file name of the image
It provides text alternatives when images cannot be seen
It is required only when width and height are missing

question: Given .menu li a { color: gold; }, which part of the selector directly targets the clickable element?
options: .menu
li
a
color

question: With box-sizing: border-box; applied, what does width: 300px represent?
options: Only content width, excluding padding and border
Content plus padding and border combined
Content plus margin but not padding
Only border thickness

question: When should you prefer CSS Grid over Flexbox?
options: When aligning items in a single row or column only
When you need two-dimensional layout control across rows and columns
When you want to avoid media queries entirely
When styling text color in navigation links

question: What is the main benefit of adding an event listener instead of inline onclick attributes in HTML?
options: It guarantees better performance in all cases
It separates structure from behavior and improves maintainability
It prevents users from opening developer tools
It automatically validates form fields

question: What does document.querySelector('#result') return if no matching element exists?
options: An empty object
false
undefined
null

question: Why is async/await often preferred over deeply nested callbacks for asynchronous code?
options: It makes code easier to read and handle errors with try/catch
It forces all asynchronous operations to run in parallel
It removes the need for network error handling
It converts asynchronous functions into synchronous ones

question: Which heading structure is best practice for page semantics?
options: Use many <h1> tags for visual emphasis only
Start at <h3> if the text looks better
Use headings in logical order to reflect content hierarchy
Avoid headings and use bold paragraphs instead

question: Why is a mobile-first approach considered a responsive best practice?
options: Because desktop users do not need responsive design
Because starting with small screens encourages simpler, scalable layouts
Because media queries are not needed afterwards
Because pixel units become invalid on mobile devices
